calls.calloutTimeouts: **Note:** You do not need to remove a session, after 30 seconds of inactivity, a session is automatically garbage collected.
calls.calloutMid: **Note:** In the API, `mid` refers to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/RTCRtpTransceiver/mid, which is a way to reference media tracks in the Web Platform. It is a user-agent defined integer, in the form of a string. (This inconsistency between string vs integer comes from https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5888#section-4, where it's a integer, but in the Web Platform defined as a DOMString https://w3c.github.io/webrtc-pc/#dom-rtptransceiver-mid)
calls.callsApiDocsCardDetail: Harness the power of our API with comprehensive documentation and code examples
calls.copySecretNowWarning: Make sure to copy your API Token now. You won’t be able to see it again.

calls.gettingStartedWithCallsDetail: Calls does not ship as a client-side SDK, but rather as a fully customizable [HTTPS API]() for simple RPC commands and server events. This enables you to choose how each client action (i.e. joining or leaving a room) maps to an API call.
calls.gettingStartedWithCallsCardDetail: Begin your journey by building your first Calls application, complete with a demo and live code
calls.gettingStartedWithRealtimeCardDeta: Begin your journey by building your first Realtime application, complete with a demo and live code
calls.gettingStartedWithCallsExampleEcho: Copy and paste the code snippet below into an html file to create your first calls web-app. In this example, you will see two local tracks (audio and video) being pushed to Cloudflare Calls, and then pulled back for playback.
calls.gettingStartedWithRealtimeExampleE: Copy and paste the code snippet below into an html file to create your first Realtime web-app. In this example, you will see two local tracks (audio and video) being pushed to Cloudflare Realtime, and then pulled back for playback.
calls.playDemoPermissions: You will be prompted for camera and mic access
calls.secretDemoWarning: When deploying to production, your API token should be kept server-side.
calls.serverlessSfuApplicationDescriptio: Receive multiple media or datachannels from clients and forward to other clients to scale WebRTC apps
calls.turnServiceKeyDescription: Use Cloudflare as a relay point for traffic between WebRTC clients when communication is obstructed by NATs or firewalls
calls.turnServiceKeyGettingStartedExampl: You need to generate short-lived credentials for each TURN user. In order to create credentials, you should have a back-end service that uses your TURN Token ID and API token to generate credentials. It will need to make an API call like this:
calls.turnServiceKeyGettingStartedRespon: The JSON response below can then be passed on to your front-end application to be used when creating the RTCPeerConnection:
calls.showDemoDetail: You will be prompted for camera and mic access
calls.turnSfuTagline: Build real-time WebRTC apps using the Cloudflare Network. Never worry about scaling or regions again.
calls.tagline: Build real-time apps using the Cloudflare Network.
calls.realtimeSubscribeDescription: Use Cloudflare Realtime to build real-time apps without worrying about scaling or regions. Cloudflare Realtime serverless SFU (selective forwarding unit) allows you to build video conferencing apps, a fanout delivery system for broadcasting media or anything in between. Cloudflare Realtime TURN Server allows you to navigate through NATs and Firewalls with your WebRTC apps.
calls.subscribeDescription: Use Cloudflare Calls to build real-time apps without worrying about scaling or regions. Cloudflare Calls serverless SFU (selective forwarding unit) allows you to build video conferencing apps, a fanout delivery system for broadcasting media or anything in between. Cloudflare Calls TURN Server allows you to navigate through NATs and Firewalls with your WebRTC apps.
calls.callsTaglineWelcome: Think of the Calls selective forwarding unit (SFU) as a "Switchboard" you can program. An SFU is a system that connects users with each other in real-time apps by intelligently managing and routing video, audio and data between the participants. Each Calls application is a separate environment, and users can only communicate with other users in the same application.
calls.realtimeTaglineWelcome: Think of the Realtime selective forwarding unit (SFU) as a "Switchboard" you can program. An SFU is a system that connects users with each other in real-time apps by intelligently managing and routing video, audio and data between the participants. Each Realtime application is a separate environment, and users can only communicate with other users in the same application.
calls.contactAdminToEnable: Contact your admin to enable Calls.

calls.kit.sessionDetails.participantsLiv: Number of live participants currently in the call.

calls.kit.participantDetails.name: Name
calls.kit.participantDetails.dataNotAvai: Contact support if you think this is a mistake
calls.kit.participantDetails.platformToo: Type of device being used - desktop, tablet, mobile.
calls.kit.participantDetails.osTooltip: Operating system & version of the participant's system
calls.kit.participantDetails.browserTool: Browser & version of the participant's system.
calls.kit.participantDetails.sdkTooltip: SDK name & version used by the participant, in this call.
calls.kit.participantDetails.poorOutgoin: Other participants are seeing poor video from this participant?
This participant is experiencing CPU constraints or limited upload bandwidth. Check the Outgoing Video graphs for red quality limitation markers indicating bandwidth or CPU bottlenecks.
calls.kit.participantDetails.poorIncomin: This participant is seeing poor quality video from others?

This participant has limited download bandwidth, unstable network, or insufficient CPU/GPU power. Check incoming video graphs for high frame drops or low resolution. If only specific participants appear poor quality, check those participants' outgoing video reports.
calls.kit.participantDetails.frozenVideo: This participant is seeing frozen/stuttering video from others?

Video freezes occur when network conditions force quality switches. Check incoming video graphs for frequent frame drops and resolution changes. This participant likely has unstable download bandwidth or CPU constraints preventing smooth playback.
calls.kit.participantDetails.audioGlitch: This participant is hearing robotic voice, noise, or audio dropouts?

Audio glitches indicate packet loss or network instability. Check the incoming audio "Robotic Voice / Noise / Dropout" graph for concealment events. High packet loss or jitter values confirm network issues. If from specific participants, check their outgoing audio reports.
calls.kit.settings.cfSfuDescription: RealtimeKit runs on Cloudflare SFU, a global media engine running across Cloudflare's 330+ data centers, delivering lower latency, improved routing, and reduced cost.
calls.kit.settings.cfOnlyBannerDescripti: This app uses the Cloudflare SFU media engine by default. No migration needed. We recommend upgrading RealtimeKit SDKs at least every 3 months for new features, performance improvements, and bug fixes.
calls.kit.settings.mediaEngineDescriptio: Your app needs to be migrated to the Cloudflare SFU - a global media engine running across Cloudflare's 330+ data centers with lower latency, improved routing, and reduced cost.
calls.kit.settings.completeByDeadline: Complete your migration before June 30th 2026 to avoid service interruptions.
calls.kit.settings.completeByDeadlineOve: The migration deadline has passed. Complete your migration immediately to avoid service interruptions.
calls.kit.settings.sdkVersionsDescriptio: Update to these exact versions before switching. They support both the old and new engine so your app continues to work during the transition.
calls.kit.settings.requiredSdkVersionsRo: Required SDK versions during rollback window
calls.kit.settings.sdkVersionsDescriptio: Stay on these exact versions while the rollback window is active. They support both the old and new engine so your app continues to work if you need to revert.
calls.kit.settings.sdkWarning: Using newer SDK versions without first switching the media engine will cause unexpected behavior such as blank recordings.
calls.kit.settings.rollbackDuringMonitor: Monitor metrics, error rates, and recordings to ensure everything works as expected.
calls.kit.settings.rollbackDuringRevert: If issues arise, use the revert button to switch back to the Legacy Media Engine.
calls.kit.settings.rollbackDuringSdkVers: Stay on the required SDK versions listed above.
calls.kit.settings.rollbackAfterPermanen: The switch becomes permanent and the revert option disappears.
calls.kit.settings.rollbackAfterUpgradeS: Upgrade to the latest SDK versions for the best performance and newest features.
calls.kit.settings.switchedBannerDescrip: Your app has been moved to the Cloudflare SFU. You have a 72-hour window to monitor and revert if needed.
calls.kit.settings.switchedRollbackMonit: Monitor metrics, error rates, and recordings during this window.
calls.kit.settings.switchedRollbackUseRe: If issues arise, use the revert button to switch back to the Legacy Media Engine.
calls.kit.settings.switchedRollbackPerma: Once the rollback window closes, the switch is permanent and the revert option disappears.
calls.kit.settings.switchedRollbackUpgra: Once the rollback window closes, upgrade to the latest SDK versions for the best performance and newest features.
calls.kit.settings.migrationCompleteBann: Your app has been permanently migrated to the Cloudflare SFU. The 72-hour rollback window has closed. We recommend upgrading RealtimeKit SDKs at least every 3 months for new features, performance improvements, and bug fixes.
calls.kit.settings.apiDomainInstruction: Make sure to use api.cloudflare.com for all RealtimeKit API calls instead of api.dyte.io, api.cluster.dyte.in, or api.realtime.cloudflare.com.
calls.kit.signUp.title: RealtimeKit
calls.kit.signUp.description: Build real-time WebRTC apps using the Cloudflare Network. Never worry about scaling or regions again.
calls.kit.createApp.title: Create app
calls.kit.createApp.description: Set up a new app to power your real-time video, audio, and collaboration experiences.
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteApp.title: Delete app
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteApp.description: Deleting this app will permanently remove it and all associated meetings, sessions, presets, recordings, and webhook configuration. If this app is in production, your live integrations will stop working immediately. This action cannot be undone.
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteApp.confirmation: To confirm, type the app name
calls.kit.dialogs.deletePreset.title: Delete preset
calls.kit.dialogs.deletePreset.descripti: Deleting this preset is permanent and cannot be undone. Participants using this preset will fail to initialise the SDK.
calls.kit.dialogs.deletePreset.confirmat: To confirm, type the preset name
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteWebhook.title: Delete webhook
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteWebhook.descript: Deleting this webhook is permanent. Once deleted you won't receive events for this webhook on your endpoint.
calls.kit.dialogs.deleteWebhook.confirma: To confirm, type the webhook name
calls.kit.dialogs.toggleMeetingStatus.ma: Marking this meeting as active will allow participants to join again.
Marking this meeting as inactive will cause all future meeting joins to fail. The meeting will not be usable until re-activated.
calls.kit.dialogs.toggleMeetingStatus.co: To confirm, type the meeting title
calls.kit.dialogs.createAppDialog.title: Create App
calls.kit.dialogs.createAppDialog.descri: An App is a workspace for your RealtimeKit resources and configuration. Use separate Apps for staging and production.
calls.kit.dialogs.createPresetDialog.des: A Preset defines a participant's role and experience in a meeting — their permissions, controls, and UI theme. Different participants can use different Presets within the same meeting.
calls.kit.dialogs.storage.introText1: You can setup your cloud storage to auto-transfer recorded meetings to it.
calls.kit.dialogs.storage.introText2Defa: By default, Cloudflare will only store your recordings for
calls.kit.dialogs.storage.introText2Suff: , after which they expire and are inaccessible.
calls.kit.dialogs.storage.introText3: Setting up external storage ensures your recordings are preserved long-term.
calls.kit.dialogs.storage.doneDescriptio: All recorded meetings will now be automatically transferred to your cloud storage account. If you want to disable this feature, you can edit or delete the storage config.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.urlHint: Must start with https:// or http://
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescMeeti: Fired when the first participant joins a meeting.
Fired 1 minute after all the participants leave the call.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescParti: Fired whenever a participant joins a meeting.
Fired whenever a participant leaves a meeting.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescRecor: Fired whenever there is a status update for a recording.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescLives: Fired whenever there is a status update for a live stream.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescChatS: Fired when chat messages of a meeting are available after it has ended.
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescTrans: Fired when the meeting ends, and consists of the transcript (if any).
calls.kit.dialogs.webhook.eventDescSumma: Fired after the meeting summary is generated, after the meeting has ended.

calls.kit.dialogs.createMeeting.demoCta: Or try a demo meeting by joining via dashboard.

calls.billing.enterpriseBanner.admin: You have access to a trial of Cloudflare Calls. Once you begin using Calls at scale, your Account Manager will follow up to discuss adding it to your existing contract.
calls.billing.enterpriseBanner.adminReal: You have access to a trial of Cloudflare Realtime. Once you begin using Realtime at scale, your Account Manager will follow up to discuss adding it to your existing contract.
calls.billing.enterpriseBanner.nonAdmin: You have access to a trial of Cloudflare Calls. Once you begin using Calls at scale, your Account Manager will follow up with your account admin to discuss adding it to your existing contract.
calls.billing.enterpriseBanner.nonAdminR: You have access to a trial of Cloudflare Realtime. Once you begin using Realtime at scale, your Account Manager will follow up with your account admin to discuss adding it to your existing contract.
calls.billing.contactYourAdminToAddCalls: Contact your account admin to add Calls subscription
calls.billing.selfServeBannerDescription: You have until , to add a subscription to your account. While your current Serverless SFU and TURN Server apps will still function, you will be unable to create additional apps until you subscribe.
calls.billing.openBetaEndedBannerTitle: Subscribe to create Serverless SFU and TURN Server applications
calls.billing.openBetaEndedBannerDescrip: You need subscribe to create additional Serverless SFU and TURN Server apps. Your existing applications and keys will still function.
calls.billing.activationPendingDescripti: Your Realtime subscription purchase completed, but account activation is still processing. Do not purchase again while activation is pending.
calls.billing.productDetails.includedFre: This is a monthly usage-based model and you will be charged only when you exceed the included usage limit.
calls.billing.productDetails.addCallsSub: Add Calls subscription to my account
calls.billing.productDetails.addRealtime: Add Realtime subscription to my account
calls.billing.productDetails.disclaimer: By clicking “Add Calls subscription to my account”, you are completing this transaction and agreeing to our [Terms](https://www.cloudflare.com/terms/) and [Privacy Policy](https://www.cloudflare.com/privacypolicy/). Your subscription automatically renews and will be billed to your payment method on file, until canceled. You may cancel anytime in [Billing Subscriptions]() at least a day before each renewal date. For Services subject to usage based billing, you will be charged based on your use of the Services during the period specified by Cloudflare.
calls.billing.productDetails.disclaimerR: By clicking “Add Realtime subscription to my account”, you are completing this transaction and agreeing to our [Terms](https://www.cloudflare.com/terms/) and [Privacy Policy](https://www.cloudflare.com/privacypolicy/). Your subscription automatically renews and will be billed to your payment method on file, until canceled. You may cancel anytime in [Billing Subscriptions]() at least a day before each renewal date. For Services subject to usage based billing, you will be charged based on your use of the Services during the period specified by Cloudflare.
calls.moq.title: MoQ
calls.moq.subtitle: Provision MoQ relays on Cloudflare’s network to deliver real-time media with QUIC-based, sub-second latency.
calls.moq.yourRelaysDescription: Each relay is a logically isolated MoQ scope with its own publish and subscribe tokens.
calls.moq.emptyTitle: Provision a MoQ relay to get started
calls.moq.emptyDescription: A relay is your own isolated space on Cloudflare’s network for sending and receiving real-time media over MoQ. When you create one, you get a publish token and a subscribe token that work with any MoQT-compatible client.
calls.moq.upsellDescription: Provisioned relays require Cloudflare Realtime. Enable it to create and manage relays with their own publish and subscribe tokens. You can keep using the public test relays below in the meantime.
calls.moq.getStarted.title: Provision MoQ relays on Cloudflare
calls.moq.getStarted.description: MoQ relays carry live audio and video over Cloudflare’s network. Enable Cloudflare Realtime to provision your own relays. Each relay has a publish token and a subscribe token, and you don’t run any servers or pick a region.
calls.moq.getStarted.feature2: A publish token and a subscribe token for each relay
calls.moq.getStarted.freeUsage: Cloudflare Realtime includes 1,000 GB of data transfer free every month.
calls.moq.getStarted.askAdminDescription: An account admin needs to enable Cloudflare Realtime before you can provision relays.
calls.moq.publicTestRelaysDescription: Open endpoints for protocol experimentation, with no authentication required. Use them for client development, and provision a relay above for production traffic.
calls.moq.benchDescription: Load test a relay by scaling up subscribers and watching live throughput and latency. Works with Draft-14 relays only.
calls.moq.cli.title: Connect with moq-rs
calls.moq.cli.intro: Publish and subscribe from the command line with moq-pub and moq-sub from cloudflare/moq-rs. The token is supplied in the connection path and identifies the relay scope.
calls.moq.cli.publishDescription: Pipe fragmented MP4 into moq-pub with your publish + subscribe token.
calls.moq.cli.subscribeDescription: Read a stream with your subscribe-only token.
calls.moq.cli.tokenNote: Token values are shown only once, when the relay is created or after a rotation. If you no longer have yours, rotate the token to reveal a new value.
calls.moq.create.title: Create MoQ relay
calls.moq.create.description: Choose a name for this relay. Publish and subscribe tokens are generated and shown once on success.
calls.moq.tokenReveal.title: Save your tokens
calls.moq.tokenReveal.description: These tokens are shown only once. Store them somewhere safe. If you lose them, you’ll need to rotate them.
calls.moq.tokenReveal.publishSubscribeDe: Full access. Can publish and subscribe.
calls.moq.tokenReveal.subscribeDescripti: Read-only. Can subscribe but not publish.
calls.moq.detail.statusConnectedTooltip: At least one namespace is registered on this relay.
calls.moq.detail.configurationDescriptio: Control how this relay handles subscriptions and upstreams.
calls.moq.detail.upstreamsDescription: Fall back to upstream MOQT server publishers when a subscribe doesn’t match a local publisher.
calls.moq.detail.upstreamsUrlsHelp: In priority order. Cloudflare will try each URL in turn until one succeeds.
calls.moq.detail.rotateTokenDescription: The existing token is immediately invalidated. Active sessions using it will be disconnected and clients must switch to the new value.
calls.moq.detail.rotateRevealDescription: Save this value now. It is only shown once. The previous token has been invalidated.
calls.moq.detail.connectionDescription: Use any MoQT-compatible client and connect to a Cloudflare relay endpoint with your token. The scope is identified by the token, not the URL.
calls.moq.detail.relayEndpointsDescripti: Choose the endpoint that matches the MoQ Internet Draft your client implements.
calls.moq.detail.authTokenDescription: Pass your token as a bearer credential during MoQT session setup.
calls.moq.detail.deleteDialogDescription: Deleting this relay is permanent. Active connections will be terminated and any clients using its tokens will lose access.
calls.moq.detail.notFoundDescription: This relay no longer exists or you do not have access to it.
calls.moq.errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
calls.moq.errors.limitReached: You have reached the relay limit for this account. Delete an existing relay before creating another.
calls.moq.errors.nameRequired: Please enter a name for the relay.
